verdict.answer_first: A: starts with the actionable answer, "Truncate it in place rather than deleting and recreating it," then immediately gives `: > /path/to/logfile`.
verdict.decisiveness: A: commits to "The simplest way" and gives one primary command before equivalents; B adds a longer detour including `tail -c 10M ...`.
verdict.actionability: A is faster to act on right now: it gives `: > /path/to/logfile` and `sudo truncate -s 0 /path/to/logfile` without the extra, partly distracting workaround section.
